definitions.text: Any system by which time is divided into days, weeks, months, and years.
examples: The three principal calendars are the Gregorian, Jewish, and Islamic calendars.

definitions.text: A means to determine the date consisting of a document containing dates and other temporal information.
examples: Write his birthday on the calendar hanging on the wall.
The club has a busy calendar this year.

definitions.text: To set a date for a proceeding in court, usually done by a judge at a calendar call.
examples: The judge agreed to calendar a hearing for pretrial motions for the week of May 15, but did not agree to calendar the trial itself on a specific date.

definitions.text: To enter or write in a calendar; to register.
